content.text:
Simple machines are tools that make work easier! They don't reduce the amount of work, but they change how we do it — letting us use less force or move things in a better direction.

There are 6 types of simple machines:

⚙️ 1. Lever — A stiff bar that rests on a pivot point (called a fulcrum). A seesaw is a lever! Levers help lift heavy objects with less force.

🎡 2. Wheel and Axle — A wheel attached to a rod. A doorknob and bicycle wheel are examples. They make turning easier.

⛵ 3. Pulley — A wheel with a rope around it. Used to lift heavy things upward by pulling down instead. Flagpoles and cranes use pulleys.

📐 4. Inclined Plane — A slanted surface (a ramp!). Instead of lifting something straight up, you push it up a ramp using less force. Wheelchair ramps and loading docks are inclined planes.

🔩 5. Wedge — Two inclined planes put together, making a pointed edge. Used to split or cut things apart. Axes, knives, and doorstops are wedges.

🔧 6. Screw — An inclined plane wrapped in a spiral. Screws hold things together and can lift objects (like a screw jack that raises a car). Jar lids also use screws!

Engineers combine simple machines to make COMPOUND MACHINES — like bicycles, scissors, and cranes.

diagrams.alt: Illustrations of six simple machines: lever, wheel and axle, pulley, inclined plane, wedge, and screw

experiment.instructions: Use the ramp (inclined plane) to roll the heavy box up! Compare how hard it is with and without the ramp.

combinations.resultLabel: The box rolls up the ramp easily! The inclined plane spreads the work over a longer distance, so you need less force at any one moment. This is why ramps are used instead of stairs for heavy loads!

practiceQuestions.prompt: A seesaw is an example of which simple machine?
practiceQuestions.hint: A seesaw rests on a pivot point in the middle — what simple machine has a fulcrum?

practiceQuestions.prompt: What simple machine is used to lift a flag on a flagpole?
practiceQuestions.hint: A flagpole has a rope and a wheel — what simple machine does this describe?

practiceQuestions.prompt: An axe that splits wood is an example of a:
practiceQuestions.hint: A wedge has a pointed edge used to push apart or cut through material.

questions.prompt: Which simple machine is a slanted surface used to move heavy objects?
A bicycle is an example of a:

options.text: Compound machine made of simple machines

questions.prompt: What is the pivot point of a lever called?
A jar lid uses which simple machine?
Simple machines make work easier by:

options.text: Removing the need for any force
Letting us use less force or move things more conveniently
Adding extra energy to the system
